title: Grounding-verification boundary (D): contradiction-hybrid + κ-taxonomy + live retrieval — RETRIEVAL-BOUND

contradiction_hybrid_gate.arm: admission=lexical + reject=NLI-contradiction, vs lexical incumbent (sealed C1 snapshot, pre-registered)
contradiction_hybrid_gate.why: On uninformative title-only C1 evidence the NLI-contradiction detector has nothing to fire on, so the hybrid degenerates to lexical. Reference NLI-entailment arm = -0.098 (over-abstains).

two_family_taxonomy.finding: the two NLI families fail DIFFERENTLY: grok LLM-NLI over-admits (hallucinates entailment on uninformative evidence); deberta cross-encoder conservatively abstains. κ=0.477.

live_retrieval.result: attempted for real; evidence returned for ~2/8 claims (scholarly/DOI only), and it is BIBLIOGRAPHIC METADATA (titles/DOI records), NOT fact-bearing sentences; other claim types timed out / returned nothing
live_retrieval.implication: live retrieval does not solve the evidence-quality bottleneck for factual entailment; it supports structural checks (does this DOI/work exist) not sentence-level support/refute

unifiedConclusion: Grounding verification on this stack is RETRIEVAL-BOUND, not mechanism-bound. On uninformative evidence (C1 fixtures titles; keyless live metadata) NO entailment method (NLI/hybrid/lexical) beats the lexical screen. The FEVER win (NLI 0.96 vs coherence 0.65) required sentence-level GOLD evidence. The productive lever is fact-bearing sentence retrieval, not the verifier head. NLI stays a validated-vs-coherence primitive + optional fail-closed backend; the contradiction-hybrid is a safe additive drop-in (ties lexical when evidence is thin, and would add contradiction-catching when evidence is informative).
